channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Impact of Fear-Based Learning Environments on Student Success
item.description: Education experts and child development specialists are warning that fear-based learning environments harm students' academic performance. Fear creates a hostile atmosphere that hinders cognitive deve...

item.title: US-Iran conflict escalates as airstrikes and retaliatory attacks disrupt Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The United States launched airstrikes against Iran following an Iranian strike on a container ship in the Strait of Hormuz, which set it ablaze and left one crew member missing. Iran retaliated by att...

item.title: Shakib and Zari Hassan Reunite After Breakup Drama
item.description: Zari Hassan and Shakib Cham Lutaaya, who previously announced their split after a five-year marriage, have been spotted together in Uganda. The couple shared playful moments on social media, with Shak...

item.title: 71 Terrorists Killed in Ongoing Operation Shaban in Balochistan
item.description: Security forces in Balochistan have killed 71 terrorists during Operation Shaban, according to state-run Radio Pakistan. The operation, launched after a July 7 attack that killed 27 police personnel, ...

item.title: Governor's Driver Injured in Police Shooting During Rally in Ol Kalou
item.description: Murang'a Governor Irungu Kang'ata revealed that his driver, Gitari, was shot by police during chaos at a Linda Mwananchi Rally in Ol Kalou, Nyandarua County. The incident occurred after his delegation...

item.title: Senate Votes on Sara Duterte's Impeachment: Legal Dispute Over 16-Vote Threshold
item.description: Senator-judge Francis 'Chiz' Escudero has stated he would welcome a legal challenge to his ruling that at least 16 affirmative votes are required to convict Vice President Sara Duterte in her impeachm...

item.title: 15 Korean Skincare Products That Have Gone Viral on TikTok
item.description: This article highlights 15 Korean skincare products that have gained significant popularity on TikTok due to their effectiveness in improving skin texture, reducing acne, and providing hydration. Prod...

item.title: Uttarakhand faces severe monsoon impact with 69 roads closed and landslides disrupting transport
item.description: Uttarakhand is grappling with severe monsoon-induced disruptions as 69 roads remain closed due to landslides and heavy rainfall. Emergency services have been placed on high alert to manage the crisis,...

item.title: Argentina Advances to Semifinals with Messi's Continued Excellence
item.description: Argentina's national football team has advanced to the semifinals of an international tournament, with captain Lionel Messi achieving another milestone in his career. The team's commanding performance...

item.title: Kurdish smuggler's asylum claim and council housing link revealed
item.description: A convicted people-smuggling ring leader, Jamal (real name unknown), has been identified as residing in a Leicester council flat for two years after being released from prison in France. French offici...

item.title: Springbok Women's Resilience Shown in Drawn Series Against US Eagles
item.description: The Springbok Women's two-Test series against the US Eagles highlighted their progression as a top-tier rugby team, despite a 26-19 defeat in Pretoria. Coach Swys de Bruin praised the squad's resilien...

item.title: PDP Faction Backed by Wike Submits Candidate Names to INEC for 2027 Elections
item.description: The faction of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) aligned with Minister of the Federal Capital Territory, Nyesom Wike, has successfully uploaded the names of its presidential candidate, running mate, ...

item.title: Taylor Swift's Wedding Reveals Uncomfortable Moments and Guest Reactions Amid Gossip
item.description: The article delves into the aftermath of Taylor Swift's high-profile wedding, highlighting both the celebratory aspects and the controversies that emerged. Initially described as a 'fairytale' event, ...

item.title: Iran and US escalate conflict over Strait of Hormuz control
item.description: The article details escalating tensions between Iran and the US over control of the Strait of Hormuz. Iran claims to have closed the strategic waterway after US strikes on Iranian targets, while the U...

item.title: Sweating Isn't the Only Indicator of Effective Strength Training
item.description: The article explores the misconception that sweating during strength training is a reliable measure of workout effectiveness. Experts explain that sweating is primarily a cooling mechanism, not an ind...

item.title: Chiltern Vital Group and Cambridge Atomworks to Build Odin Microreactor Prototype at Berkeley Site
item.description: A significant development in nuclear energy innovation is unfolding as Chiltern Vital Group (CVG) and Cambridge Atomworks have signed a letter of intent to construct the Odin microreactor prototype on...

item.title: Philippines commemorates 10th anniversary of West Philippine Sea arbitral ruling
item.description: The Subic Bay Metropolitan Authority (SBMA) chair, Eduardo Jose Aliño, highlighted the significance of the 2016 arbitral ruling in the West Philippine Sea as a 'beacon of hope' during a commemorative ...

item.title: Springboks' Tactical Versatility and Quan Horn's Adaptability Secured Victory Against Scotland
item.description: The Springboks showcased their tactical versatility during a crucial match against Scotland, with coach Rassie Erasmus opting for a 6-2 bench split that tested the adaptability of his players. Key sub...

item.title: MeitY scrutinizes Meta's handling of child sexual abuse content on Instagram
item.description: The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has received Meta's response to its notice regarding advertisements and content allegedly promoting child sexual exploitation and abuse m...

item.title: South Africa investigates Interpol fugitive Ndodana Tshuma's entry and arrest
item.description: The South African Police Service (SAPS) is conducting an investigation into how Interpol fugitive Ndodana Tshuma, accused of murdering his wife and two children in England, may have entered South Afri...
